onboardingSelectDrawing: Select a drawing tool from the menu{{1}}
onboardingDrawReservoir: Click anywhere to place a reservoir.
onboardingDrawTank: Click anywhere to place a tank.
onboardingStartPipe: Click on an existing node to draw a pipe, or click anywhere to start with a new junction.

onboardingCtrlPipe: Hold {{1}} and click to end and start a new pipe immediately.
onboardingDrawPipe: Click to define vertices. Finish at a junction or double-click to add one.
onboardingRedrawLink: Click to define new vertices for the selected link. Finish at a junction or double-click to add one.
onboardingConnectCustomerPoints: Click on a pipe to connect the customer point.
onboardingShiftConnectCustomerPoints: Hold {{1}} to connect the customer points at cursor location.
onboardingStartPump: Click on an existing node to draw a pump, or click anywhere to start with a new junction.
onboardingStartPumpEmpty: Click anywhere to start drawing a pump.
onboardingCtrlPump: Hold {{1}} and click to end and start a new pump immediately.
onboardingDrawPump: Click to define vertices. Finish at a junction or double-click to add one.
onboardingStartValve: Click on an existing node to draw a valve, or click anywhere to start with a new junction.
onboardingStartValveEmpty: Click anywhere to start drawing a valve.
onboardingCtrlValve: Hold {{1}} and click to end and start a new valve immediately.
onboardingDrawValve: Click to define vertices. Finish at a junction or double-click to add one.
onboardingSelectAsset: Select any asset to view and modify its properties.
nothingSelectedTitle: Select or search an asset to view its properties
nothingSelectedClickToSelect: Use the select tool and click on an asset to view and edit its details.
nothingSelectedAreaSelect: You can also select multiple assets with the area select tool and compare their properties.
nothingSelectedSearch: Or search assets by their label to quickly find and select them.
onboardingRunningModel: Running a model and viewing results
onboardingRunSimulation: Press Simulate button to run a hydraulic simulation.
onboardingMap: Switch to the map tab to add visualizations of hydraulic results.
onboardingMoveNode: Drag the node to change its position.
onboardingMoveCustomerPoint: Drag the customer point to change its position.
onboardingEditVertices: Click and drag vertices to reshape the link.
onboardingAutomaticCalculations: Elevations and lengths are assigned automatically.
onboardingAutomaticUpdates: Elevations and lengths are updated automatically.

mldDescription: SI metric with flows expressed in Ml/d
cmhDescription: SI metric with flows expressed in m³/h
cmdDescription: SI metric with flows expressed in m³/d
gpmDescription: US customary with flows expressed in gal/min
cfsDescription: US customary with flows expressed in ft³/s

afdDescription: US customary with flows expressed in acft/d
startNotificationLanguageDescription: We thought you might enjoy using epanet-js in your language. It's currently in beta, and it might not be fully accurate. You can switch languages at any time.
dropInp: Drop an .INP file here to open the model.
dropProjectOrInp: Drag your project or INP model here.
inpMissing: Invalid file type. Use an .INP file.
couldNotOpenRecentFile: Could not open the recent file. Try Open INP option.

Only one INP file is allowed at a time.
The first one will be used.
playbackSpeedWarningTooFast: Results may not be visible before the next update at this speed.
playbackSpeedWarningSlow: Updating the map is taking over 1s per step — playback will be slow.
welcomeIntro: We're creating a new way to build and run hydraulic models—right from your browser in a modern, web-based, open source platform. Quickly open, edit, and analyze your networks on your computer without installing any software. All simulations run directly on your machine, keeping your data private—no cloud required.
welcomeNewHere: New here? Check out our {{1}} to learn the basics in just a few minutes!
welcomeExploreWithSamples: Explore the app by opening a sample network
demoUKStyleDescription: UK style network with metric units and flow in l/s.
demoUSStyleDescription: North American style network with US units and flow in GPM.
checkConnectionAndTry: Please check your internet connection and try again.
missingCoordinatesDetail: Your INP is missing required coordinates. Please ensure all assets include geocoded information using the WGS84 standard.
inpNotFullySupportedDetail: Expand the summary to view unsupported features and track their progress on the roadmap.
newFeaturesEveryDay: We're continuously adding new features and improvements! Stay informed about the latest updates by subscribing to our newsletter!
reservoirPatterns: Reservoirs with time patterns ({{1}} reservoirs using first pattern value only)
tankCurves: Tanks with volume curves ({{1}} tanks using fixed diameter)
pumpPatterns: Pumps with time patterns ({{1}} pumps using constant speed)
pcvCurves: PCV valves with characteristic curves ({{1}} valves using setting value only)
alertInpOutputSubtitle: Your INP file was created with another application.
alertInpOutputDetail: Please note that some sections and settings may be formatted differently or omitted. We encourage you to save a copy instead of overriding the original content.
alertExportInp: Exporting to INP may result in lost data
alertExportInpDetail: The INP format doesn't support all the features available in our app. To ensure no data is lost, we recommend saving your work as a Project first.
projectSavedInfo: Your work is now saved as a Project
projectSavedInfoBody: We've upgraded how saving works! Your file is now saved as a native Project, which preserves your exact workspace state and allows us to store more advanced details.
projectSavedInfoFootnote: Need an INP file? You can still use File > Export to generate one at any time.
alertScenariosNotSavedDetail: Only the main model will be saved. Your scenarios will remain available in this session but will be lost when you close the app. Full scenario persistence is coming soon.
alertNetworkRequiredDetail: You need to select or create a network before you can start building a scenario.

simulationSettings.title: Simulation settings
simulationSettings.hydraulicTimestepDesc: How often a new hydraulic state of the network is computed. If greater than either the pattern or report time step it will be automatically reduced.
simulationSettings.reportingTimestepDesc: The time interval between which output results are reported.
simulationSettings.patternTimestepDesc: The interval between time periods in all time patterns.
simulationSettings.qualityTimestepDesc: The time step used to track changes in water quality throughout the network. The default is 1/10 of the hydraulic time step.
simulationSettings.ruleTimestepDesc: The time step used to check for changes in system status due to activation of rule-based controls between hydraulic time steps. The default is 1/10 of the hydraulic time step.
simulationSettings.epsZeroValues: Values must be greater than zero.
simulationSettings.globalDemandMultiplierDesc: Adjust the values of baseline demands for all junctions and all demand categories. For example, a value of 2 doubles all baseline demands, while a value of 0.5 would halve them.
simulationSettings.demandModelDesc: Specifies whether a demand driven analysis (DDA) or a pressure driven analysis (PDA) should be made. Under DDA full nodal demands are always met even if negative pressures result. PDA assumes that demand varies between 0 and its full value as a power function of nodal pressure.
simulationSettings.minimumPressureDesc: The pressure below which no demand can be delivered under a pressure driven analysis.
simulationSettings.requiredPressureDesc: The pressure required to supply a node's full demand under a pressure driven analysis. It must be at least 0.1 pressure units higher than the minimum pressure.
simulationSettings.pressureExponentDesc: The power to which pressure is raised when computing the demand delivered to a node under a pressure driven analysis.
simulationSettings.emitterExponentDesc: The power to which the pressure at a junction is raised when computing the flow issuing from an emitter.
simulationSettings.backflowAllowedDesc: Specifies if back flow through an emitter (i.e., flow into the network) is allowed.
simulationSettings.trialsDesc: The maximum number of trials used to solve network hydraulics at each hydraulic time step of a simulation.
simulationSettings.accuracyDesc: The convergence criterion that determines when a hydraulic solution has been reached. The trials end when the sum of all flow changes from the previous solution divided by the total flow in all links is less than this number.
simulationSettings.unbalancedModeDesc: Determines how the simulation proceeds if the hydraulic state fails to converge within the maximum number of trials. Stop halts the entire analysis. Continue allows the simulation to proceed with a warning. If Extra Iterations are provided, the engine will attempt additional trials with link statuses (like pumps and valves) held fixed to reach a solution.
simulationSettings.unbalancedExtraTrialsDesc: The number of additional trials allowed to reach convergence if the 'Continue If Unbalanced' option is active. During these iterations, link statuses are locked to prevent the simulation from 'oscillating' between states. If convergence is still not reached, the simulation continues with a warning.
simulationSettings.headErrorDesc: An optional convergence criteria that augments the standard Accuracy setting. It sets the maximum allowable difference between the headloss calculated from link flow and the headloss calculated from node pressures. A value of 0 indicates that no headloss error limit is applied.
simulationSettings.flowChangeDesc: An optional convergence criterion that augments the standard Accuracy setting. It specifies the largest allowable change in flow for any network element (links, emitters, or pressure-driven demands) between iterations. A value of 0 indicates that no flow change limit is applied.
simulationSettings.checkFreqDesc: This sets the number of solution trials that pass during hydraulic balancing before the status of pumps, check valves, flow control valves and pipes connected to tanks are once again updated.
simulationSettings.maxCheckDesc: This is the number of solution trials after which periodic status checks on pumps, check valves, flow control valves and pipes connected to tanks are discontinued. Instead, a status check is made only after convergence is achieved.
simulationSettings.dampLimitDesc: This is the accuracy value at which solution damping and status checks on PRVs and PSVs should begin. Damping limits all flow changes to 60% of what they would otherwise be as future trials unfold. The default is 0 which indicates that no damping should be used and that status checks on control valves are made at every iteration.
simulationSettings.viscosityDesc: Ratio of the kinematic viscosity of the fluid to that of water at 20 deg. C (1.0 centistokes or 0.94 sq ft/day).
simulationSettings.specificGravityDesc: Ratio of the density of the fluid being modeled to that of water at 4 deg. C.
simulationSettings.qualitySimulationTypeDesc: Type of water quality parameter being modeled. Choices include: None (no quality analysis), Chemical (compute concentration), Age (estimate water age), Trace (percent flow from node).
simulationSettings.qualityChemicalNameDesc: Enter the actual name of the chemical being modeled (e.g., Chlorine).
simulationSettings.qualityMassUnitDesc: Mass units used to express concentration. Choices are mg/L or ug/L. Units for Age and Trace analyses are fixed at hours and percent, respectively.
simulationSettings.qualityTraceNodeDesc: ID label of the node whose flow is being traced. Applies only to flow tracing analyses.
simulationSettings.traceNodeRequired: A valid node label is required
simulationSettings.toleranceDesc: Smallest change in quality that will cause a new parcel of water to be created in a pipe. A typical setting might be 0.01 for chemicals measured in mg/L as well as water age and source tracing.
simulationSettings.diffusivityDesc: The ratio of the modeled chemical's molecular diffusivity to that of chlorine at 20°C. This only applies to wall reactions; set to zero to ignore mass transfer effects.
simulationSettings.reactionBulkOrderDesc: The power to which concentration is raised when computing bulk flow reaction rates. Use 1 for first-order, 2 for second-order, or a negative number for Michaelis-Menton kinetics.
simulationSettings.reactionWallOrderDesc: Determines if wall reactions are first-order (1) or zero-order (0). This dictates whether the reaction rate depends on the current concentration or remains constant.
simulationSettings.reactionTankOrderDesc: The reaction order used for all tanks in the network. It typically defaults to first-order (1) or zero-order (0) kinetics.
simulationSettings.reactionGlobalBulkDesc: The default reaction rate coefficient for all pipes, representing reactions within the fluid itself. Use a positive value for growth, a negative value for decay, or 0 for no reaction.
simulationSettings.reactionGlobalWallDesc: The default reaction rate coefficient for all pipes, representing reactions at the pipe wall. Use a positive value for growth, a negative value for decay, or 0 for no reaction.
simulationSettings.reactionLimitingPotentialDesc: Defines a concentration limit for reactions. When set, reaction rates become proportional to the difference between the current concentration and this value, rather than the total concentration.
simulationSettings.reactionRoughnessCorrelationDesc: A factor used to adjust the Global Wall Coefficient based on individual pipe roughness. Set to zero to ignore the effect of roughness on reaction rates.
simulationSettings.reportEnergyDesc: Include a summary of energy usage and cost for each pump in the simulation report.
simulationSettings.energyGlobalEfficiencyDesc: The default energy efficiency (%) for all pumps in the network. This value is used to calculate the power consumed by the pump based on its flow and head. You can override this for individual pumps if they have specific manufacturer-provided efficiency curves.
simulationSettings.energyGlobalPriceDesc: The default energy cost per kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) used across the entire simulation. This value is multiplied by the calculated energy consumption of each pump to determine the total operating cost.
simulationSettings.energyGlobalPatternDesc: The ID of a time pattern that describes how the energy price varies throughout the day (e.g., for "Time-of-Use" billing). If left blank, the Global Price remains constant.
simulationSettings.energyDemandChargeDesc: An additional charge based on the maximum power (kW) consumed by the network during the simulation period. This represents the "peak demand" fee often charged by electric utilities.
simulationSettings.flowUnitsDesc: Sets the units in which flow rates are expressed.
simulationSettings.pressureUnitsDesc: Sets the units in which pressure is expressed.
simulationSettings.headlossFormulaDesc: Selects a formula to use for computing head loss for flow through a pipe.
simulationSettings.statusReportDesc: Determines whether a hydraulic status report should be generated. If Yes is selected the report will identify all network components that change status during each time step of the simulation. If Full is selected, then the status report will also include information from each trial of each hydraulic analysis.

failedToFetchElevationExplain: Elevation data cannot be retrieved, so 0 will be assigned.
elevationNotAvailableExplain: It wasn't possible to retrieve the elevation for this point. Using 0 instead.
noInternetExplain: Some features may not be available.
mapOfflineModeExplain: The application is now using a blank background.
failedToLoadTileJSON: A TileJSON layer failed to load. The tile server may be down
headsUpSmallScreen: Heads up! Not optimized for small screens
smallScreenExplain: It looks like you're trying to access the app from a smaller device. This application is currently in preview mode for this screen size, and some editing features may not be available.
continueAnywayExplain: You can proceed, but the experience may not be ideal, and some features might be missing.
watchQuickDemoExplain: View a short demonstration showing the full app experience on desktop.
visitLandingPageExplain: Learn more about the app and what it can do.
somethingWentWrongMessage: Something went wrong. If the error persists, contact support.
tryAgainOrSupport: Please try again, and if the error persists, contact support.
planChangesFromSupportExplain: Please contact our support team to modify your subscription.
upgradeSuccessfulExplain: Thanks for upgrading — your new features are now unlocked.
cannotRenderMap: Cannot render map on your browser
cannotRenderMapExplain: The map requires WebGL, which could not be initialized in your browser. This issue can sometimes occur after suspending or putting your computer to sleep. Please try closing your browser and reopening the page in a new browser window.
cannotRenderMapAction: If the problem continues after updating your browser and graphics drivers, please contact {{1}} for assistance.

videoTutorial.title: Customer points import tutorial
videoTutorial.description: Learn how to import customer points by watching the tutorial above or reading the <guideLink>step-by-step guide</guideLink>.

attributesMapping.title: Attributes mapping
attributesMapping.description: Map the properties from your GIS data to the customer point attributes

dataPreview.title: Data preview
dataPreview.selectPrompt: Select a demand property to preview the data.

labelSelector.label: Label
labelSelector.description: Labels will be trimmed to {{1}} characters

messages.skippedRowsWarning: These rows were skipped during import. Only valid customer points will be processed.

replaceOption.title: Replace existing demands with customer demands (default)
replaceOption.description: Set all base demands at junctions to zero and use only the customer point demands. Recommended when customer data is the main source of demand.

addOnTopOption.title: Add customer demands on top of existing demands
addOnTopOption.description: Keep the current base demands and add customer point demands on top. Use this if you want to include both existing and customer-based demands.

timePattern.title: Time pattern
timePattern.description: Optionally apply a time pattern to all imported customer demands.

allocationStep.title: Customers allocation
allocationStep.description: Define rules for connecting customer points to pipes. You can specify multiple max distance and max diameter pairs to control how customer points are allocated to the network.

table.noRulesMessage: No allocation rules defined. Click "Add rule" to create your first rule.

dataSource.title: Add from file
dataSource.noValidPointsError: No valid customer points found in the selected file.
dataSource.coordinateValidationError: All coordinates must be expressed in WGS84 (EPSG:4326) projection, or include a CRS definition in the GeoJSON

importCustomerPointsWarning.explain: Your current customer points will be permanently deleted and replaced with the points from your new import.
importCustomerPointsWarning.question: Are you sure you want to proceed?

networkReview.title: Network Review
networkReview.description: Run a series of checks to validate your network's topology and ensure data quality.

connectivityTrace.title: Connectivity trace
connectivityTrace.description: Identify disconnected sub-networks and verify each has a water supply source

orphanAssets.title: Orphan assets
orphanAssets.description: Find assets that are disconnected from your network
orphanAssets.emptyMessage: There are no orphan assets in your network. Well done!

proximityAnomalies.title: Proximity check
proximityAnomalies.description: Finds junctions located close to nearby pipes that may need to be connected.
proximityAnomalies.emptyMessage: No nearby nodes found - you can increase the search distance to find more.

crossingPipes.title: Crossing pipes
crossingPipes.description: Find pipes that intersect without a connecting node.
crossingPipes.emptyMessage: No crossing pipes found in your network

privacyBanner.title: Protecting your privacy
privacyBanner.description: We use tracking technologies to understand how the app is used and to improve your experience. To comply with privacy regulations, we need your consent to collect this data.
privacyBanner.seePrivacyPolicy: For more details, please see our
privacyBanner.essentialDataNotice: To ensure the app works properly, we automatically collect anonymous data about page views and use feature flags to enable and manage new features. This information is essential for the app's basic functionality and cannot be disabled.
privacyBanner.productAnalyticsDescription: We collect anonymous usage data to understand how features are used and to improve performance and functionality. This information does not personally identify you.
privacyBanner.errorReportingDescription: We collect crash and error reports to fix bugs and prevent issues. This may include details about your device and what was happening in the app when the error occurred.

paywall.title: Upgrade to access scenarios
paywall.description1: Scenarios let you explore "what-if" changes without duplicating your model.

paywall.plans: Scenarios are available on paid plans.
paywall.demo: Try scenarios for free with a demo network, or explore our paid plans.
